page: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped from 2 physical AMD EPYC cores running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with 240 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark evaluations reveal strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core performance. In single-core stress-ng tests, the instance achieves a top-tier score of 3612.09, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also perform exceptionally well, with gzip compression reaching 39.73 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 17.58 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is more constrained, likely due to the shared CPU allocation; multi-core stress-ng scores and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth and database operations, such as Redis SET performance at 8.1 million operations per second, consistently align with mid-tier averages.

The primary tradeoff of the vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is its shared CPU allocation, which introduces potential resource contention under sustained multi-threaded workloads, as reflected in the lower multi-threaded benchmark tiers. Qualitatively, this shared-resource model offers high resource density and cost efficiency for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, lightweight database operations, and development environments. It is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing speeds for 7B models drop to a poor 15.94 tokens per second.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor (x86_64 architecture, 2.0 GHz) and 16.0 GB of RAM. It includes 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the top 10%. However, due to its shared CPU allocation, multi-threaded performance and multi-core decompression fall into average and weak categories. This server offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and lightweight database operations, while being less suited for sustained multi-threaded compute or intensive machine learning tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and fast NVMe storage for cost-efficient everyday workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC processors at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory paired with 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and stress-ng benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation optimized for cost-efficient, burstable workloads
Suitable for static web serving, development environments, and light databases
